data.title: WEX posts $673.8M revenue, $2.22 diluted EPS in Q1
data.description:
WEX reported first-quarter results with total revenues of $673.8 million and diluted net income per share of $2.22, reflecting year-over-year revenue growth and higher earnings per share. Net income rose to $77.7 million while operating income remained essentially flat. The company noted growth across Mobility, Benefits and Corporate Payments as drivers of the top-line increase.

Financial Highlights

  • Total revenues: $673.8 million (up from $636.6 million prior year)
  • Gross Profit: $393.7 million
  • Operating Income: $158.2 million (essentially flat vs $157.3 million prior year)
  • Net Income: $77.7 million (up from $71.5 million prior year)
  • Net income attributable to shareholders per share (Diluted): $2.22 (up from $1.81)

Business Highlights

  • Revenue growth drivers: Growth was driven by Mobility, Benefits and Corporate Payments segments.
  • Channel & volume shifts: Company-wide transaction volume increased 8% to $58.1 billion, led by Corporate Payments (+10% volume) and Mobility fuel spend (+6%).
  • Brand & product momentum: Benefits HSA deposits increased, supporting fee and other revenue; SaaS accounts rose approximately 4%, reinforcing recurring service demand.
  • Operational investments: Continued investments in sales and product teams increased sales and marketing spend to support growth and partner expansion.
  • Risk and operations: Higher credit and fraud provisioning in Mobility and elevated receivables increased operational funding needs and working capital activity.

Original SEC Filing: WEX Inc. [ WEX ] - 10-Q - Apr. 23, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: D.R. Horton 10-Q: Revenues $7.56B Qtr, Diluted EPS $2.24
data.description:
D.R. Horton reported quarter revenues of $7,558.1 million and diluted net income per share attributable to D.R. Horton of $2.24, reflecting a year-over-year decline in margins and revenue. Net income for the quarter was $647.9 million attributable to shareholders, with year-to-date diluted EPS of $4.27 and attributable net income of $1,242.7 million. The company cited pressure on home sales margins and elevated incentives as drivers of the change versus prior periods.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenues: $7,558.1 million (Quarter), $14,445.0 million (Year-to-date); consolidated revenues decreased 6% year-over-year.
  • Gross profit – total homebuilding: 19.8% of homebuilding revenues (Quarter), 20.0% (Year-to-date); home sales gross margin 20.1% (Quarter) down from 21.8% prior year.
  • Income before income taxes (Operating income equivalent): $867.4 million (Quarter), $1,665.4 million (Year-to-date); pre-tax margin 11.5% YTD versus 14.2% prior YTD.
  • Net income: $658.0 million (Quarter total); net income attributable to D.R. Horton: $647.9 million (Quarter), $1,242.7 million (Year-to-date attributable).
  • Diluted net income per share attributable to D.R. Horton: $2.24 (Quarter), $4.27 (Year-to-date); diluted EPS declined versus the prior year.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue & sales momentum: Home sales revenue declined modestly (roughly 2–5%) while closings remained stable; net sales orders increased 7–11%, indicating resilient demand.
  • Channel & product mix: Approximately 85% of revenue derived from single-family detached homes; rental and multifamily closings fell, while build-to-rent single-family activity expanded.
  • Pricing & incentives: Average selling prices decreased about 2–3%; management is using elevated sales incentives and mortgage rate buydowns to support affordability and drive order flow.
  • Land & inventory strategy: 77% of lots are controlled via purchase contracts; the company continues to prioritize Forestar and third-party lots to optimize lot supply.
  • Operational efficiency & execution: Focus on cost control, targeted SG&A discipline, management of construction starts, and regional execution to help preserve margins amid market pressures.

Original SEC Filing: HORTON D R INC /DE/ [ DHI ] - 10-Q - Apr. 23, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Newsmax lawsuit against Fox moved to Florida after judge faults 'forum-shopping'
data.description:
By Mike Scarcella

A federal judge on Thursday ordered Newsmax’s antitrust lawsuit against Fox Corp transferred back to Florida, saying the conservative broadcaster improperly abandoned an earlier case there against its larger rival and refiled it in Wisconsin after an adverse ruling.

Here are the details:

  • Newsmax accuses Fox of suppressing competition in the market for the conservative-leaning pay TV. Fox violated antitrust law by coercing distributors to exclude competitors such as Newsmax or to restrict their reach, the lawsuit alleges.

  • A Florida judge last year dismissed Newsmax’s first lawsuit based on a technical defect, and Newsmax refiled it in Wisconsin. The dismissal order from U.S. District Judge Aileen Cannon was not on the merits of the lawsuit.

  • Wisconsin-based U.S. District Judge William Conley’s order on Thursday said Newsmax engaged in "forum shopping." The judge said Wisconsin has no meaningful connection to the dispute, and that Newsmax is based in Florida.

  • “Although obvious, plaintiff offers no explanation why it dismissed the Florida case. Much less obvious is why it refiled here,” Conley wrote in his order.

  • Fox has denied any wrongdoing and moved to dismiss Newsmax's claims. In a prior statement, Fox said "Newsmax cannot sue their way out of their own competitive failures in the marketplace to chase headlines simply because they can’t attract viewers."

Read more:

Newsmax denies 'forum shopping' in antitrust lawsuit against rival Fox

Newsmax sues Fox again, expands lawsuit after Florida case dismissed

Newsmax to refile lawsuit against Fox after judge faults 'shotgun' complaint

data.title: Grace Therapeutics receives FDA Complete Response Letter for GTx-104 NDA, plans resubmission
data.description:
Grace Therapeutics received a Complete Response Letter from the FDA for its GTx-104 NDA citing CMC and non-clinical items and intends to resubmit.

Key Highlights:

  • FDA issued a Complete Response Letter for the GTx-104 NDA for treatment of aneurysmal subarachnoid hemorrhage.
  • CRL cites Chemistry, Manufacturing & Controls and non-clinical issues: leachables, toxicology assessments, and CMO manufacturing deficiencies.
  • FDA did not request additional clinical data, and the company believes cited issues can be addressed in a resubmission.
  • Grace Therapeutics intends to request a Type A meeting with the FDA to clarify the path forward and next steps.

Original SEC Filing: Grace Therapeutics, Inc. [ GRCE ] - 8-K - Apr. 23, 2026

data.title: Bulls charge ahead - AAII
data.description:
Key points:
  • Main US indexes fall; Nasdaq off most, down ~0.8%
  • Tech is weakest S&P 500 sector; Utilities sector leads gainers
  • Dollar up; US crude gains >2%; gold, bitcoin both off ~0.5%
  • US 10-year Treasury yield rises to ~4.32%

BULLS CHARGE AHEAD - AAII

Optimism among individual investors about the short-term outlook for the U.S. stock market surged in the latest American Association of Individual Investors (AAII) Sentiment Survey. With this, both pessimism and neutral sentiment decreased.

Meanwhile, nearly half of investors surveyed said that they think other investors are "too bullish."

AAII reported that bullish sentiment, or expectations that stock prices will rise over the next six months, charged ahead by 14.3 percentage points to 46.0% (highest reading since 49.5% on January 15). Bullish sentiment is above its historical average of 37.5% for the first time in 10 weeks.

Bearish sentiment, or expectations that stock prices will fall over the next six months, tumbled 8.4 percentage points to 34.4%. Bearish sentiment is above its historical average of 31.0% for the 11th consecutive week.

Neutral sentiment, or expectations that stock prices will stay essentially unchanged over the next six months, lost 5.9 percentage points to 19.5%. Neutral sentiment is "unusually low" and is below its historical average of 31.5% for the 92nd time in 94 weeks.

The bull-bear spread surged 22.7 percentage points to 11.6% from –11.1% last week. The bull-bear spread is above its historical average of 6.5% for the first time in 11 weeks.

In this week's special question, AAII asked its members if they think other investors are too bullish or too bearish right now.

Here is AAII's graphic showing how they responded.

data.title: BNP Paribas Hikes Intel Stock Price Target Ahead of Earnings
data.description:
Intel rose about 1% on Tuesday after BNP Paribas upgraded the chipmaker ahead of its April 23 first-quarter results.

Analyst David O'Connor lifted Intel to Neutral from a lower rating and raised his price target to $60. He said the stock's 86% gain this year has pushed it beyond fundamentals and that expectations have risen after improved data points tied to Intel's 14A manufacturing process.

O'Connor said agentic artificial intelligence, software that can act on tasks with limited human input, is driving stronger demand for server processors. He said cloud customers are scrambling for supply, which may support utilization and pricing for Intel's chips.

The analyst added that Intel remains well positioned in the server market and pointed to its recent repurchase of Apollo's 49% stake in its Fab 34 plant in Ireland. He still sees Intel trailing AMD and Arm in some areas, but said those concerns may matter less through the rest of the year.

data.title: 3F, built on Morpho, raises $4 million to offer leveraged exposure to tokenized assets
data.description:
3F, a vault protocol built on Morpho, has raised $4 million in funding to offer leveraged exposure to tokenized real-world assets.

That total includes a $750,000 pre-seed round and a $3.3 million seed round, Sonya Kim, co-founder of 3F, told The Block. The pre-seed fundraising began in July 2025 and closed in November 2025, while the seed round started in November 2025 and closed in March 2026, Kim said.

Both rounds were structured as simple agreements for future equity, or SAFEs, with token warrants on a one-to-one equity-to-token conversion basis, Kim said. She declined to disclose the valuation.

The seed round was led by Maven 11, with participation from F-Prime, Fidelity’s venture arm, Susquehanna Crypto, GSR, Gate Ventures, and others.

Pre-seed investors included Steakhouse Financial, Rune Christensen, co-founder of Sky (formerly MakerDAO), and Sam MacPherson, co-founder of Phoenix Labs, the team behind Spark protocol. Maven 11 general partner Mathijs van Esch has taken an observer seat at 3F's board, Kim said.

What is 3F?

Built on Morpho, a decentralized lending protocol, 3F enables users to take leveraged exposure to tokenized real-world assets through a "one-click" process.

"Users select a supported RWA and a leverage factor, and the protocol handles the full position build: coordinating short-term bridge financing to acquire the underlying RWA, supplying it as collateral on Morpho and borrowing stablecoins against it to repay the bridge," Kim said.

She explained that building such positions typically requires a process known as "looping," where users repeatedly buy an asset, post it as collateral, borrow against it, and reinvest. While this can be done in a single transaction for crypto-native assets using flash loans, it becomes slow and complex for real-world assets due to settlement delays, Kim said.

"For a T+1 asset, for instance, building a 5x position through roughly 20 loops would take 20 days to enter and another 20 days to unwind," Kim said, adding that this exposes users to market and operational risks.

3F handles this process behind the scenes, completing it within a single settlement cycle of the underlying asset.

"Today, professional funds run manual loops on Morpho or Aave against these assets, but the trades are operationally arduous," Kim said. With 3F, the process becomes more efficient and lower risk to execute, she noted.

At the same time, Kim acknowledged that leverage comes with risks and trade-offs. These include the risk of yield spreads narrowing if borrowing costs rise, slower entry and exit due to settlement timelines, smart contract and regulatory risks, and exposure to underlying credit events.

Launch timeline

3F will initially support JAAA, a tokenized AAA collateralized loan obligation or CLO fund from web3 asset manager Anemoy, sub-managed by Janus Henderson and tokenized by Centrifuge.

3F expects demand for tokenized assets to grow as leveraged exposure becomes available. "A tokenized fixed income fund yielding 6%, financed at 4%, mathematically delivers 10-14% return at 3-5x leverage," it said. "That kind of profile gives on-chain capital a substantive reason to buy tokenized funds that it doesn’t have today."

As for 3F's business model, Kim said the platform will generate revenue through management fees on total capital deployed and performance fees on leveraged returns.

3F currently operates with a team of six and plans to hire in credit underwriting, technology, and security, Kim said. The new funding will support development as it moves toward launch, with a private beta opening this week and a broader launch expected in the second quarter, she added.

The Funding newsletter: Stay on top of the latest crypto VC funding and M&A deals, news, and trends with my free bi-monthly newsletter, The Funding. Sign up here!

Disclaimer: The Block is an independent media outlet that delivers news, research, and data. As of November 2023, Foresight Ventures is a majority investor of The Block. Foresight Ventures invests in other companies in the crypto space. Crypto exchange Bitget is an anchor LP for Foresight Ventures. The Block continues to operate independently to deliver objective, impactful, and timely information about the crypto industry. Here are our current financial disclosures.

data.title: dForce to Launch CFX Rewards Campaign on April 24th
data.description:
dForce will launch a rewards campaign on Unitus, Conflux on April 24th. Over the next two weeks, suppliers will receive 120,000 CFX and borrowers of AxCNH will receive 55,000 CFX.

Refer to the official tweet by DF:

dForce
@dForcenet

🎉 Rewards confirmed on Unitus, Conflux!

Starting this Friday, April 24th, @Conflux_Network will keep distributing 120,000 CFX for Suppliers as well as 55,000 CFX for Borrowers of AxCNH on https://t.co/LaT7Py4G7s for the next two weeks!

👉 Supply or borrow AxCNH on Unitus andâ€¦ pic.twitter.com/rc2O1ChsxX

Apr 23, 2026

DF Info

dForce is a fully integrated and open financial platform that includes several protocols that offer decentralized finance (DeFi) services. The project offers users a wide range of financial services, such as staking, yield farming, lending and borrowing.

dForce's main product is dForce Lending. dForce Lending is a decentralized lending platform. Users can provide their cryptocurrencies as collateral, which allows them to earn interest, as well as borrow other cryptocurrencies. The whole process is carried out automatically through smart contracts, eliminating the need for direct interaction between the lender and the borrower.

When depositing assets into the pool, users receive collateral tokens reflecting their share in the pool. Borrowers can borrow funds up to a certain percentage of the value of their collateral tokens. In case of incomplete coverage of the loan by collateral, the position of the borrower is subject to liquidation. Interest rates on the dForce Lending platform are formed automatically in accordance with the supply and demand for a certain asset, which guarantees efficient and transparent pricing.

DF is a native token, that is used to participate in the governance of the protocol and receive rewards for participating in staking.

data.title: Siacoin Released Monthly Report for March 2026
data.description:
Siacoin released its monthly report for March 2026 on April 23rd. The report covers major updates across Sia Storage App, the SDK stack and the Grants Program.

Refer to the official tweet by SC:

Sia Foundation
@SiaFoundation

Check out the latest State of Sia to learn about major improvements from March:

🟢 Sia Storage App

🟢 indexd

🟢 Sia-storage-sdk

🟢 Sia-sdk-rs

🟢 hostd

🟢 renterd

🟢 s3d

🟢 Grants Program

Read more about these updates 🔗 https://t.co/hKf6FMWODI

Apr 23, 2026

SC Info

Sia serves as a trustless and secure marketplace for cloud storage, where users can rent out their unused storage space. The primary objective of Sia is to position itself as the backbone storage layer of the internet, aiming to compete with major cloud storage providers like Amazon, Google, and Microsoft by offering more competitive storage rates due to its decentralized nature.

In the Sia network, files are divided into 30 encrypted segments, each stored with a different host for redundancy purposes. The relationships between those who upload and those who host files are governed and enforced through smart contracts on Sia’s blockchain. Siacoin facilitates transactions within this network, with renters paying hosts in SC for storage services, and hosts using SC as collateral within these smart contracts to ensure reliability and trust.

Siacoin (SC) is the native utility token of Sia, a decentralized cloud storage platform built on blockchain technology. Siacoin has an unlimited supply to match the potentially limitless demand for data storage and transactions on the network. New Siacoins are minted as mining rewards via a proof-of-work algorithm, with the mining reward now fixed at 30,000 SC per block. The introduction of The Sia Foundation has led to a doubling of the block reward, with half going to the Foundation. Siacoins are removed from circulation when hosts forfeit their tokens due to bad actions. The Sia team plans to implement a proof-of-burn mechanism where hosts must burn a portion of their earnings to demonstrate their legitimacy. The Sia development team pre-mined approximately 30 million SC before public release, but the team and investors hold less than 0.1% of the total Siacoin supply.

data.title: Teck: No Plans for Valuable Royalty on Fourmile Gold Project — Market Talk
data.description:
1437 ET - Teck Resources for now has no plans for its interest in the Fourmile project in Nevada, which Barrick Mining estimates is one of this century's most significant gold finds and would be a key asset its plans to separately list its North American operations. As about any plans to monetize the royalty held on Fourmile, Teck Chief Executive Jonathan Price tells analysts the miner has no specific plans right now. "We recognize that that's a valuable asset that we have here in the portfolio," he says. Price says there remains a lot of technical work still be done around the Fourmile development, which will further inform the value of the royalty that his company holds. (robb.stewart@wsj.com)

1432 ET - IBM's decision not to lift its full-year outlook was in line with the expectations of Morgan Stanley analysts, who say it's a reasonable choice given uncertainty from the war in Iran. Investors may have been expecting an upward revision to the full-year guide, they write, leading to pressure on the stock after the print. But IBM typically doesn't adjust its guidance after the first quarter and the most recent print doesn't change the stock's overall story. Management's commentary on the conference call also suggests some risk to growth in Europe, despite strong Middle East growth in 1Q first quarter. "These factors all support why IBM deferred to being prudent/keeping their full year guide unchanged tonight," the analysts write. Shares slide 8.3%.(elias.schisgall@wsj.com)

1405 ET - Keurig Dr Pepper is hoping to get in on a trend taking the U.S. by storm: Dirty soda, or soda spiked with flavored syrups and creams. Earlier this month, the beverage giant relaunched its Dr Pepper Creamy Coconut limited time offering after its initial run in 2024, looking to tap into consumer interest in dirty sodas, Chief Executive Tim Cofer says during a call with analysts. "We expect that to be a big hit this year, capitalizing on dirty sodas," he says. Restaurants have also jumped on the trend recently, with players like Taco Bell andTGI Fridays offering their own versions. (kelly.cloonan@wsj.com)

1355 ET [Dow Jones]--IBM's stock is trading down despite acceleration in Red Hat revenue, as overall software growth in constant currency missed Wall Street expectations and investors hoped for an upward revision to the company's full-year guidance, JPMorgan analysts write in a note. Despite strong growth in both Red Hat and the company's data business, aided by early contributions from IBM's acquisition of Confluent, automation growth returned to low double digits, weighing on the segment as a whole. The analysts cut their price target to $270 from $283, citing incremental risk from the software and consulting segments. Shares are down 9.4% to $228.20. (elias.schisgall@wsj.com)

1356 ET - Parts of Southwest Airlines' latest earnings report give it a leg up amid weakness among other carriers, Raymond James analysts say. "Against a peer group that has generally disappointed this earnings season, Southwest's non-fuel cost execution and outlook stand out as particularly impressive," the analysts say in a note. The carrier's first-quarter operating expenses--excluding aircraft fuel and related taxes expense, special items and profit sharing--increased 3.9%. And while the company's guidance was below the analysts' forecast, the midpoint is somewhat ahead of consensus estimates, while those from competitors like Alaska Air, Delta and United generally came in below expectations, the analysts say. (kelly.cloonan@wsj.com)

1343 ET - Southwest Airlines' transformation initiatives are delivering results but at a bit of a slower pace than the market expected, UBS analysts say in a note. The carrier's 1Q revenue per available seat mile, and its 2Q guidance, were underwhelming compared to estimates, but the company's cost performance in the latest quarter topped expectations, the analysts say. They note that Wall Street was likely looking for Southwest to have a stronger lead in quarterly revenue growth over competitors like Delta and United given it has several transformation strategies in place. (kelly.cloonan@wsj.com)

1335 ET - Keurig Dr Pepper says some of the headwinds hurting its coffee business should ease over the rest of the year. Pressures from higher green coffee costs and tariffs, as well as a decline in coffee pod shipments, should ease slightly in the current quarter and moderate further in the back half of the year as costs come down and short-term trade inventory dynamics normalize, finance chief Anthony DiSilvestro says during a call with analysts. "Cost inflation will meaningfully ease in the second half, and our innovation and commercial programming will begin to kick in, and we should see some top line improvement," he says during a call with analysts. "Based on current coffee prices, this could be a tailwind for us going into 2027." (kelly.cloonan@wsj.com)

1312 ET - Keurig Dr Pepper has refined its strategy around promotions in its core carbonated soft drink business to draw shoppers looking for affordability, Chief Executive Tim Cofer says. The beverage giant is looking to offer low prices in key channels, while still ensuring that it continues to offset pressure to its bottom line from rising costs, he says during a call with analysts. He says the volume the company has been seeing in carbonated soft drinks this year shows the value that its providing to consumers with affordable pack sizes, even as it raises some prices to offset inflation. (kelly.cloonan@wsj.com)

1257 ET - The oneworld Alliance already had more than a dozen members — now it's bringing Hawaiian Airlines into the fold. Customers who are members of Atmos Rewards, the combined loyalty program for Alaska Airlines and Hawaiian Airlines, can now earn and redeem points across the oneworld network. The airline's top tier guests will have access to oneworld Priority benefits, including a network of nearly 700 airport lounges. In recent years, airlines have adjusted their loyalty programs to reward big spenders rather than budget-conscious customers, The Wall Street Journal has reported. (chris.kuo@wsj.com)

1235 ET - After a recent rough patch, Metro's in-line 2Q results won't be enough to quell investor concerns. TD Cowen's Michael Van Aelst says last quarter, Metro shares fell 5.5% the day of the results as Ontario distribution center freezer issues, holiday timing and later start to cold season pushed same-store sales and EPS growth below expectations. While 2Q results were positive, sequential Food same-store sales growth "trends softened and the outlook statement points to 3Q headwinds from a strike at the Laval, Quebec produce distribution center." To make matters worse, consumer demand appears to be softening, and competitive pressure is mounting. The bad offsets the good. "We do not think these in-line Q2 results will ease investor concerns around the latter," Van Aelst says. (adriano.marchese@wsj.com)

1225 ET - In recent years, soaring demand for artificial intelligence and cloud computing has caused a massive expansion of data centers across the U.S., boosting the semiconductor business of Texas Instruments. Growth in its data center business has accelerated in the past three quarters, according to UBS analysts. The company's data center revenue surged about 90% year-over-year, analysts say, with the segment expected to continue to grow in the coming quarters. In particular, the company expects its application-specific engagements for AI to ramp up in the second half of 2026, according to Susquehanna analysts.(chris.kuo@wsj.com)

1222 ET - The finish line to Honeywell's restructuring is in sight. The company is in the process of spinning off several businesses in an effort to streamline operations and carve itself into three publicly traded companies. It has now set a date of June 29 to spin off Honeywell Aerospace, after previously completing the spinoff of its advanced-materials business, Solstice Advanced Materials, in October. Honeywell additionally agrees to sell its warehouse and workflow-solutions business to private-equity firm American Industrial Partners, after earlier this week agreeing to sell its productivity solutions-and-services business to Brady. "Following these sales and spin, we will have a more cohesive portfolio focused on three principal end markets," CEO Vimal Kapur says on a call with analysts. (connor.hart@wsj.com)

data.title: Ayco Farms: There Is No Active Cantaloupe Recall
data.description:
By Josh Beckerman

Ayco Farms said recent media coverage has incorrectly characterized a cantaloupe recall as ongoing and said cantaloupes that were distributed in December and January and voluntarily recalled never tested positive for salmonella.

According to a notice on a Food and Drug Administration website, the recall was initiated March 24 and classified as Class I, the highest risk level, on April 20.

On April 10, Ayco said inclusion in the FDA's weekly enforcement report reflected the previously completed recall. The company said at the time that "all product associated with this matter had already passed its shelf life and was no longer in commerce at the time of the March notification."

The recall included 8,302 cartons. The cantaloupes were distributed in Pennsylvania, Florida, California and New York.

Ayco Farms, of Pompano Beach, Fla., supplies melons, pineapple, asparagus and peas.

Write to Josh Beckerman at josh.beckerman@wsj.com

data.title: Aptos to Participate in AI & Blockchain Redefining Markets in New York City on April 24th
data.description:
Aptos will participate in AI & Blockchain Redefining Markets in New York City on April 24th. Aptos Labs CBO Solomon Tesfaye will represent the project at the Cornell Tech event.

Refer to the official tweet by APT:

Aptos Labs
@AptosLabs

AI & Blockchain Redefining Markets | Friday, April 24, 2026

Find Aptos Labs' CBO, @SolomonTesfaye_, bringing @Aptos to Cornell Tech in New York City alongside an incredible group of innovators and institutions 🗽

Agenda, speaker details, and tickets: https://t.co/7bQ38V7k2x pic.twitter.com/5kpLi3C9EJ

Apr 23, 2026

APT Info

Aptos is a new, independent high-performance Layer 1 project focused on delivering the safest and most scalable blockchain in the world. The Aptos blockchain leverages the Move programming language to make transactions fast and secure.

The Aptos data model enables flexible key management and hybrid custodial options. This, alongside transaction transparency prior to signing and practical light client protocols, provides a safer and more trustworthy user experience.

To achieve high throughput and low latency, the Aptos blockchain leverages a pipelined and modular approach for the key stages of transaction processing. Specifically, transaction dissemination, block metadata ordering, parallel transaction execution, batch storage, and ledger certification all operate concurrently. This approach fully leverages all available physical resources, improves hardware efficiency, and enables highly parallel execution.

data.title: Global refined copper market to swing to surplus in 2026, group says
data.description:
The global refined copper market is expected to shift to a surplus of 96,000 metric tons in 2026, reversing a previously forecast deficit of 150,000 tons, due to slower demand growth and increased secondary production, the International Copper Study Group said on Thursday.

The organization projected a surplus of 377,000 metric tons in 2027 but cautioned that geopolitical risks, including the war in the Middle East, and changes in trade flows could impact market balances.

Global refined copper usage is now expected to grow by 1.6% in 2026, down from a prior estimate of 2.1%, and by 2% in 2027.

Chinese demand is projected to rise 1.9% in 2026, with growth of 1.3% anticipated in other regions, ICSG said, adding that consumption in the European Union and Japan would remain subdued, while Asia will continue to drive global growth.

Global refined copper production is forecast to expand 0.4% in 2026, constrained by limited concentrate availability, offset in part by higher secondary output, before accelerating by 3% in 2027 as concentrate production improves and new capacity is added.

Copper mine output is expected to grow 1.6% in 2026, revised down from 2.3%, due to slower growth in the Democratic Republic of Congo, Chile, and Indonesia, as well as constraints at the Grasberg and Kamoa mines following problems in 2025.

Mine output is projected to increase by 2.3% in 2027, supported by new capacity ramp-ups, improved production in Chile and Zambia, and higher operating rates in Indonesia and the DRC.

data.title: CDC Blocks Report On COVID-19 Vaccine Effectiveness
data.description:
WASHINGTON (dpa-AFX) - The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) has blocked the publication of a scientific report on how well the COVID-19 vaccine works, according to news reports.

The Washington Post reported that the study was supposed to appear on March 19 in the CDC's weekly Morbidity and Mortality Weekly Report. The study found that the COVID-19 vaccine reduced the chances of going to the emergency room or being hospitalized by about half during the past winter.

The study had already passed the CDC's usual scientific review. But it was later delayed by acting CDC Director Dr. Jay Bhattacharya, who had concerns about how the study was carried out, the report added. However, experts noted that the same method has been used by the CDC for years to study vaccines for respiratory illnesses, including a recent flu vaccine study.

When asked about the issue, a CDC official did not comment directly on the blocked report but said the agency must follow the highest scientific standards in what it publishes, CIDRAP News reports.

'Responsible science requires careful review. Taking time to ensure analyses are methodologically sound and clearly communicated is always preferable to risking error,' the official said.

The decision is unusual because the study used the CDC's standard approach for measuring how well vaccines work. It also relied on a large research network that collects health data from nine U.S. healthcare systems to track vaccine effectiveness across different age groups.

Sources say Dr. Bhattacharya questioned a common study method called the 'test-negative design,' though officials have not clearly explained his concerns, as per CNN Health.

Even after passing internal checks and being scheduled for release, the study was officially rejected by the journal, according to one source. A spokesperson from the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services confirmed that the study was not published.

'Suppressing the standard of science on VE [vaccine efficacy] to wait for a perfect study in a system that cannot support it is not a hallmark of transparent scientific expertise,' Demetre Daskalakis, the former director of the CDC's National Center for Immunization and Respiratory Diseases, said in an email to CIDRAP News.

He further added that the cancellation of the report appears to be 'cherry picking based on the bias of the director and others at HHS who don't fully understand the importance of the methods used to assess the added benefit of vaccines in preventing poor outcomes.'

Copyright(c) 2026 RTTNews.com. All Rights Reserved

Copyright RTT News/dpa-AFX

EQUITIES

GLOBAL - Wall Street stocks were modestly lower on Thursday and crude prices extended their climb as Iran flaunted its control over the Strait of Hormuz and investors digested a host of mixed corporate earnings reports.

MSCI's gauge of stocks across the globe EURONEXT:IACWI fell 1.33 points, or 0.12%, to 1,069.98.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

NEW YORK - Wall Street's main indexes were flat on Thursday, as investors awaited clear signals on the U.S.-Iran war, while a batch of mixed earnings reignited concerns about AI-driven disruption across the software sector.

At 11:45 a.m. ET, the Dow Jones Industrial Average fell 99.59 points, or 0.20%, to 49,388.60 and the Nasdaq Composite lost 52.77 points, or 0.21%, to 24,604.80. The S&P 500 lost 2.17 points, or 0.03%, after hitting an all-time high.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

LONDON - European shares were little changed on Thursday as investor worries over collapsed U.S.-Iran peace talks and weak economic data outweighed upbeat corporate results.

The pan-European STOXX 600 index closed flat at 614.20 points.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

TOKYO - Japan's Nikkei share average reversed course on Thursday after briefly topping the 60,000 level, as investors locked in profits from an early rally as the outlook of the war in the Middle East remained unclear.

The broader Topix fell 0.76% to 3,716.38.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

SHANGHAI - Chinese and Hong Kong shares closed down on Thursday as investors turned risk-averse amid escalating tensions in the Middle East.

China's blue-chip CSI300 Index and the Shanghai Composite Index both ended 0.3% lower.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

AUSTRALIA - Australian shares ended at their lowest in more than two weeks on Thursday, driven by a broad-based sell-off across most sectors as stalled U.S.-Iran peace talks and elevated oil prices sapped risk sentiment, while energy major Santos jumped more than 3%.

The benchmark S&P/ASX 200 index fell 0.6% to 8,793.40 points, their lowest closing point since April 7.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

SEOUL - South Korean shares notched a fresh record close on Thursday, led by gains in chipmakers on strong first-quarter data.

The benchmark KOSPI rose 57.88 points, or 0.90%, at 6,475.81.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

FOREIGN EXCHANGE

NEW YORK - The dollar edged higher and was on track for a weekly gain on Thursday, as a standoff between Iran and the United States escalated tensions in the Middle East and dashed hopes for a peace deal.

The dollar index , which tracks the performance of the U.S. currency against six others, rose 0.08% to 98.69.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

SHANGHAI - China's yuan eased slightly against the dollar on Thursday, tracking broader greenback strength as simmering Middle East tensions supported safe-haven investments and lifted oil prices back above $100 a barrel.

The spot yuan opened at 6.8251 per dollar and was last trading at 6.8288 at 0239 GMT, a touch weaker than the previous late-session close.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

AUSTRALIA - The Australian and New Zealand dollars skidded on Thursday, as risk appetite took a turn for the worse after a record rally on Wall Street, while bond yields climbed along with oil prices.

The Aussie fell 0.3% to $0.7140, having edged up 0.1% overnight.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

SEOUL - The won was quoted at 1,481.0 per dollar on the onshore settlement platform , 0.05% lower than its previous close at 1,480.3.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

TREASURIES

NEW YORK - U.S. Treasuries edged higher on Thursday, in largely rangebound trading, as lingering uncertainty surrounding the ceasefire between the United States and Iran continued to complicate the assessment of risk sentiment in the market.

In midmorning trading, the benchmark 10-year yield, which moves inversely to prices, was flat on the day at 4.294% .

For a full report, click on

- - - -

LONDON - The euro zone's short-dated bond yields were on course for their fourth daily rise as tensions around the Strait of Hormuz bolstered expectations of European Central Bank rate hikes, although weak economic data earlier in the session helped keep a lid on the moves.

Germany’s 2-year yields (DE2YT=RR), more sensitive to expectations for policy rates, rose 2 basis points to 2.562%.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

TOKYO - Japanese government bond yields rose on Thursday as investors grew cautious about the progress of talks between Iran and the U.S. in the Middle East.

The 10-year JGB yield (JP10YTN=JBTC) rose 3 basis points (bps) to 2.425%.

For a full report, click on

COMMODITIES

GOLD - Gold pared some losses on Thursday on news of a potential Lebanon-Israel ceasefire extension and after Treasury yields retreated from session highs following U.S. weekly jobless claims data.

Spot gold was down 0.3% at $4,722.02 per ounce, as of 11:52 a.m. EDT (1552 GMT), after falling 1% earlier in the day to as low as $4,683.84 per ounce.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

IRON ORE - Iron ore prices eased on Thursday, as investors weighed prospects of a growing supply of the key steelmaking ingredient against higher costs stemming from the prolonged Iran conflict.

The most-traded iron ore contract on China's Dalian Commodity Exchange (DCE) closed daytime trade down 0.32% at 783.5 yuan ($114.70) a metric ton.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

BASE METALS - Copper prices retreated from multi-week highs on Thursday, as stalled U.S.-Iran peace talks, the continued closure of the Strait of Hormuz and a stronger dollar pressured prices.

Benchmark three-month copper on the London Metal Exchange dropped 0.5% to $13,368 a metric ton by 1600 GMT, after touching its highest since February 27 at $13,481.50.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

OIL - Crude futures climbed more than $1 a barrel on Thursday as the conflict between the U.S. and Iran seemed to be intensifying in the Strait of Hormuz.

Brent crude futures rose $1.62, or 1.59%, to $103.53 a barrel at 11:35 a.m.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

PALM OIL - Malaysian palm oil futures closed lower on Thursday, after three straight sessions of gains, as softer Dalian's palm olein prices triggered profit-taking.

The benchmark palm oil contract for July delivery on the Bursa Malaysia Derivatives Exchange lost 49 ringgit, or 1.06%, to 4,579 ringgit ($1,155.44) a metric ton at the close.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

RUBBER - Japanese rubber futures extended gains to a second consecutive session on Thursday, tracking firmer Thai physical prices, while higher oil prices and a weaker yen also provided support.

The Osaka Exchange (OSE) rubber contract for September delivery , was up 24.7 yen, or 1.19%, at 399.7 yen per kg.

For a full report, click on

data.title: Texas Instruments Is Maintained at Overweight by JP Morgan
data.description: This news item displays a headline only and has no other text.

data.title: IBM's Unchanged Guidance Seen as Prudent — Market Talk
data.description: IBM's decision not to lift its full-year outlook was in line with the expectations of Morgan Stanley analysts, who say it's a reasonable choice given uncertainty from the war in Iran. Investors may have been expecting an upward revision to the full-year guide, they write, leading to pressure on the stock after the print. But IBM typically doesn't adjust its guidance after the first quarter and the most recent print doesn't change the stock's overall story. Management's commentary on the conference call also suggests some risk to growth in Europe, despite strong Middle East growth in 1Q first quarter. "These factors all support why IBM deferred to being prudent/keeping their full year guide unchanged tonight," the analysts write. Shares slide 8.3%.(elias.schisgall@wsj.com)

data.title: Arabica coffee jumps nearly 4% to trade above $3 again
data.description:
Arabica coffee futures on ICE rose nearly 4% on Thursday to trade above $3 per pound - the first closing above that level since March 26 - as certified exchange stocks fall and traders show interest in receiving coffee on May's expiration.

Raw sugar was little changed, while cocoa also rose.

COFFEE

* Arabica coffee settled up 11.2 cents, or 3.9%, at $3.0035 per lb, having closed up 2.3% on Wednesday.

* "Arabica certified stocks have been reducing, now at 515,000 bags, as the influx of coffees to ICE (especially Central America's) dries up," said Ryan Delany, chief analyst at the Coffee Trading Academy (CTA).

"Volume pending inspection is a modest 20.700 bags and it hasn't been increasing so far in April," he said, referring to coffee traders send to the exchange to be certified.

* Dealers also noted strong interest from market participants to receive coffee at May expiration on ICE, with 244 lots stopped.

* Robusta coffee rose 3% to $3,507 a metric ton, having settled up 1.9% on Wednesday.

* Keurig Dr Pepper topped estimates for first-quarter sales and profit, riding on resilient demand for its beverages amid a drag from its coffee business which saw a 8% decline in volumes.

COCOA

* London cocoa settled up 27 pounds, or 1.1%, to 2,575 pounds per ton, after closing up 3.7% on Wednesday.

* The contract hit a two-month high last week.

* "It appears that a new (slightly higher) trading range is currently forming in the market," said a dealer.

* Ivory Coast's cocoa grind, a measure of demand, rose 1.4% year-on-year in March, industry data showed.

* Expectations are growing that cocoa demand will recover as prices have lost nearly three-quarters of their value since late 2024.

* New York cocoa rose 0.9% to $3,458 a ton.

SUGAR

* Raw sugar was little changed at 13.60 cents per lb, having hit a five-year low last Friday.

* "The fact that the sugar price (has fallen) despite the oil price remaining high points to negative sentiment and a significant oversupply," Commerzbank said in a note.

* Higher energy prices are bullish for sugar, because they tend to encourage cane mills to produce less sugar and more ethanol, a cane-based biofuel.

* Drier weather across parts of central Brazil, the world's top sugar grower, has helped the development of the sugarcane harvest, which is just getting underway, meteorologists said.

* White sugar rose 0.9% to $427.50 a ton.

data.title: STBA: Net income up 8% YoY, record deposits, strong capital, and ongoing share buybacks
data.description:
Net income rose 8% year-over-year to $35 million, with record deposit growth and strong capital levels. Loan balances declined due to competitive pressures, but asset quality improved and treasury management fees showed momentum. Share buybacks and M&A flexibility remain priorities.
